texto: Improving upon nature is the very essence of plant breeding, and so it goes to the heart of one of the central debates of the human condition: the relationship between humanity and nature and the degree to which the human race has a right (or indeed a responsibility) to change plant life for its own ends.
explicacao: Citação de Noël Kingsbury sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Improving upon nature is the very essence of plant breeding, and so it goes to the heart of one o…” articula amor, botany, history em registro de amor, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e termino.

frase_original: Improving upon nature is the very essence of plant breeding, and so it goes to the heart of one of the central debates of the human condition: the relationship between humanity and nature and the degree to which the human race has a right (or indeed a responsibility) to change plant life for its own ends.

texto: Sometimes shows became almost obsessively obscure, as with the gooseberry (Ribes uva-crispa) shows of nineteenth-century Britain, when workingmen in the industrial counties of northern England and the Midlands formed themselves into societies, constituted with presidents, secretaries, and stewards, for the purpose of running gooseberry shows—weight being the decisive factor. Quite why this fruit, always something of a minority taste, should become the subject of what only could be described as a cult remains a mystery.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Noël Kingsbury sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “Sometimes shows became almost obsessively obscure, as with the gooseberry (Ribes uva-crispa) show…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a trabalho, botany, history, útil em momentos de trabalho e history.
